description: Single-user MCP server that ingests NYC family-friendly events into SQLite + FTS5 and exposes them as MCP tools over streamable HTTP, gated by an OAuth 2.1 + PKCE shim. See CLAUDE.md for architecture and README.md for setup.

features.name: Phase 1 — NYC permit ingest core
features.description: Event model + stable compute_id, split SQLite stores (events + oauth) with idempotent migrations, FTS5 search, and the nyc_permitted_events (tvpp-9vvx) source with aggressive permit-registry filtering. ~700 events / 60-day window, all low_confidence.
features.evidence: tests/test_db.py, tests/test_nyc_permitted_events_parse.py green; source live in ENABLED_SOURCES.

features.name: MCP server + OAuth shim + HTTP security baseline
features.description: FastMCP over streamable HTTP at root, OAuth 2.1 + PKCE consent shim using MCP_AUTH_TOKEN, claude.ai web connector compatibility (browser-probe 200, DNS-rebinding disabled, forwarded-proto handling), and the Checkpoint C security bundle (secrets.compare_digest, redirect allowlist, consent CSP headers, per-IP rate limiter, token TTL).
features.evidence: tests/test_security_fixes.py green; see CLAUDE.md 'HTTP security baseline' and 'claude.ai web connector quirks'.

features.name: Missing-event detection (possible cancellations)
features.description: Future events that disappear from a source's feed are flagged (possibly_cancelled) rather than deleted, with four guard layers: hard-failure opt-out, circuit breaker for silent partial fetches, self-healing flag/clear, and a read-time grace window. Opt-in via Source.window_days; mommy_poppins stays excluded.
features.evidence: tests/test_missing_detection.py, tests/test_weekend_window.py green; see CLAUDE.md 'Missing-event detection'.

features.name: Phase 2 — editorial scrapers
features.description: Real descriptions, URLs, and age ranges from venue sources via the source-adder recipe (fixture -> parser -> registry -> parser test -> docs). Live: Mommy Poppins, BPL, Brooklyn Children's Museum, Green-Wood Cemetery, Prospect Park Alliance, NY Transit Museum, Brooklyn Army Terminal. Buildable backlog cleared.
features.evidence: Eight sources in ENABLED_SOURCES with matching test_<source>_parse.py fixtures; SOURCES-BACKLOG.md statuses synced. Rejected: Time Out NY Kids, Coney Island USA.

features.name: Docker packaging + deploy (Checkpoint D)
features.description: Multi-arch (amd64 + arm64) GHCR image built by docker-publish.yml, non-root uid 10001, hardcoded /data DB paths, 127.0.0.1-bound port behind Tailscale Funnel, Watchtower-scoped auto-update, TCP healthcheck, host-cron nightly ingest via docker exec.
features.evidence: Dockerfile, docker-compose.yml, docker-compose.dev.yml, .github/workflows/docker-publish.yml; see CLAUDE.md 'Docker conventions'.

features.name: Phase 3 — location enrichment, venue expansion, tech debt
features.description: Make events location-aware (geocoding + neighborhood backfill, distance-from-home filter), attach weather to upcoming outdoor events, add an indoor/outdoor heuristic flag, expand the Phase-3 venue list, and close deferred issues #4/#5/#6. Enrichment runs as a second nightly pass with caching; fetch() stays simple. Playwright adopted only as a fallback render tool (split ingest image). AI/LLM enrichment is explicitly out of scope.
features.evidence: Plan only — see PHASE-3-PLAN.md. No implementation started.

features.name: Session-handoff harness tooling
features.description: Repo-tailored init.sh, feature-list.json, progress.md, and session-handoff.md so web/remote sessions can bootstrap, verify, and hand off cleanly. Replaces the default Claude templates with project-specific content.
features.evidence: init.sh runs pytest + ruff via .venv; this file, progress.md, and session-handoff.md populated from existing root docs.
